If you have a smartphone you’re comfortable using, the easiest way to apply for SNAP is to complete the application online, then download the mobile app to submit the required documents.

Here’s the link to the online application: https://a069-access.nyc.gov/accesshra/ The mobile app is called “NYC Access HRA”.

After you complete the application questions on the website, you can connect your account to the app & use your phone camera to take pictures of docs you have to submit!

If you need to visit a SNAP/HRA office in person, you can find your local office here: https://www1.nyc.gov/site/hra/locations/snap-locations.page

* There are 2 ways to submit snap application
	* By online https://www.mynjhelps.gov/register
	* By person open this link to download application form .. https://www.nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/apply/ways/

* The easiest way to apply for SNAP is online at myDHR: https://mymdthink.maryland.gov/home/#/.
	* After submitting the application, you will be interviewed in-person or over the phone and you should recieve a response within 30 days. If you decide to submit your application at a local department of social services, a case manager can assist you in filling out missing information and completing an interview.

The easiest way to apply for SNAP is online at Georgia Gateway: https://gateway.ga.gov/access/ You will need to complete and submit the online application. You may then be asked to complete an interveiw in-person or over the phone. You should have proof of income and expenses available; however, your income and elegibility will primarily be verified through the social security number provided in the application.

* Call Met Council’s Benefits Helpline at 212-453-9532 for assistance or to schedule an appointment in person or virtually

The services of Meals On Wheels of Tampa are available to any resident in the Tampa area who is a senior or who has difficulty leaving their homes and is challenged to prepare meals.  Many recipients live alone and are handicapped, malnourished, elderly, or otherwise unable to prepare food for themselves. People of ALL incomes and ages may be eligible for meals.  Follow this link https://mowtampa.org/meal-service/  or call (813) 238-8410.

Pickup Date: When you call, we'll tell you when you can come pick up your order.

When completing a SNAP application/Redetermination, these are some of the documents we may need to 
complete your application. Call us for more information or assistance: 
 Picture I.D. 
 Social Security Card 
 Social Security award letter (if being received) 
 Pension Letter (if being received) 
 Most recent paystubs worth a month or a letter 
from your employer (if you are working) 
 Most recent BG&E bill and or other utilities that 
you are paying right now (if you pay utilities) 
 Copy of your most recent rent bill or mortgage (if 
you pay any) 
 Copy of monthly pharmacy bill (a printout 
summary, if you pay $35.00 or more out of pocket) 
 Copy of health insurance payment (if you pay out 
of pocket each month) 
 Copy of medical bills and co pays already paid 
(invoices and or bank statements, if you pay) 
 Copy of Lease Agreement (if you pay)

SNAP, formerly known as the Food Stamp Program, is the nation’s most important anti-hunger program. SNAP provides nutritional support for low-income seniors, people with disabilities living on fixed incomes, and other individuals and families with low incomes. SNAP is a federal program administered by the Florida Department of Children and Families Office of Economic Self-Sufficiency (ESS). ESS is responsible for determining eligibility for SNAP using federal guidelines. Households can use food assistance benefits to buy breads, cereals, fruits, vegetables, meats, fish, poultry, dairy, and plants and seeds to grow food for your household to eat. For more information on how to apply: https://myaccess.myflfamilies.com/

Lasagna Love feeds families, spreads kindness and strengthens community.  Sign up online & get matched to a volunteer in your community who will deliver a free home-cooked lasagna. Fulfillment time depends on where you live – it can take several weeks to receive food. Enter your zip code here to see the expected wait time:   https://lasagnalove.org/zip-code/

We do not have any qualifications to receive a meal. We understand that need comes in many forms and they are not always financial. Our core values are to feed families, spread kindness, and strengthen communities. You can visit us at https://www.lasagnalove.org/ to see more about how the process works. I've also attached a couple of resources that provide information about Lasagna Love. 

You may have times where you know of families in need. You can share the attached flyer with families, and you can also nominate families by going to https://www.lasagnalove.org/nominate/. Once a nomination has been made, Lasagna Love sends the nominated family a text with a link to sign up. It is up to the family to decide if they would like to send in their request for a meal. This allows us to verify any food allergy or dietary needs with the family. They will then be contacted directly by a volunteer once they are matched! 


Request a meal here: https://lasagnalove.org/request-a-meal/

Feast of Justice serves 11 Northeast Philadelphia zip codes: 19111, 19114, 19115, 19116, 19120, 19124, 19135, 19136, 19149, 19152, and 19154. 
* **You are only qualified to register if you live in one of these zip codes.** 
* The registration is for the full household; there can be only one registration per household.

* ID is recommended to expedite the registration process, but not required.

**Action steps to take - visit https://www.feastofjustice.org/registration** to view:

2. a **link to complete the online application**
3.  **registration hours specific to first-time guests**
4. It is recommended to schedule a registration appointment; walk-ups are served after appointments, on a first-come, first served basis during registration hours.

*Apply Online with ePASS: https://epass.nc.gov/
*Apply in Person at your county Department of Social Services (DSS) office: https://www.ncdhhs.gov/localDSS

To receive food assistance from Manna Food at Glenmont United Methodist Church:

*   **Eligibility:**
    *   You must be a Montgomery County resident.
    *   Your household income must fall below what is necessary for self-sufficiency.

*   **Scheduling an Order:**
    *   Please call Manna's main office at (301) 424-1130.
    *   Calls are accepted Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM.
    *   You must call by 12:00 PM the day *before* your desired pick-up date to ensure your order is ready.

*   **Food Pick-Up:**
    *   Bring a photo ID that includes your current Montgomery County address, or provide a document proving your current county residency.
    *   If you cannot pick up your food on the scheduled day, please call Manna to reschedule.

* Pantry services are available to anyone who resides in New York City (all 5 boroughs) or Yonkers.

**The FAQs**
* How do I get food?
	* Registration happens on-site.  Bring an ID and proof of address until you receive a client card.  They only allow one person per household (address) to become a client at a time.  If you live with someone who is already a client, they will turn you away.

* Can someone deliver the food to my house?
	* Delivery services are only available for clients who live in Inwood.  The program, Inwood Meals-on-Heels, is very limited and is reserved for clients with disabilities or serious health issues.  If you are an Inwood resident on disability (and have documentation), let them know at foodpantry@cis-nyc.org. 
 
* What do I have to bring?
	* The Cabrini Immigrant Services Food Pantry suggests that you bring a cart.   Bring an ID and proof of address until you receive a client card.  After you receive a client card, bring your client card each time you come.
 
* Do I need an appointment: 
	* No appointment necessary, but clients cannot pick up a food package two weeks in a row.
 
* What kind of food do they have? 
	* The Cabrini Immigrant Services Food Pantry offer grocery-style items such as canned food, fresh produce, frozen meat, and dry goods like pasta or rice that you can cook yourself.
 
* Do they have vegetables? 
	* The Cabrini Immigrant Services Food Pantry gives out 5-8 different types of fruits and vegetables each week.
 
* Do they have meat?
	* The Cabrini Immigrant Services Food Pantry gives out frozen meat each week.

**Peace on the Move (POTM)** offers programs to empower and equip individuals and families to move from dependency to self-sufficiency. We welcome all people and are dedicated to transforming and building better lives for individuals, families, and communities through learning and action.

Our programs include:

**Feed the Need Community Food Pantry**
Feed the Need aims to improve the quality of life for families and individuals by providing grocery relief and food boxes. The program collaborates with hunger response agencies to promote self-sufficiency and transform lives.

**Mission of Benevolence**
The Mission of Benevolence provides financial aid and resources to help individuals facing unforeseen hardships. The ministry focuses on building a foundation for self-sufficiency through temporary assistance.

**Commodity Supplemental Food Program**
The Commodity Supplemental Food Program enhances the health of low-income seniors by providing nutritious USDA foods. Through Peace on the Move, we help supplement the diets of individuals over the age of 60.

**Peace Community Teaching Garden**
The Peace Community Teaching Garden educates children and youth about healthy eating, gardening, and sustainability. It offers hands-on programs that teach kids to grow fresh produce while fostering a connection to nature.

Click [Here](https://peaceonthemove.org/all-programs) to Learn More About POTM Programs

If you are unable to pick up your food on the scheduled day, please call Manna to reschedule.

REGISTRATION IN ADVANCE IS REQUIRED

If you have trouble signing up with Plentiful, please fill out this form https://forms.gle/ruJqpe2Z8Ang2QAV8 and bring your phone number written on paper before your arrival.

1) All clients must wait on the line until called and check in inside.

A STAFF MEMBER WILL DIRECT YOU INSIDE WHEN IT IS YOUR TURN.

2)All clients must have their phone number written down or have the plentiful app
downloaded.

PLEASE NOTE WE DO NOT PROVIDE PEN & PAPER TO CLIENTS.

3) Clients will be issued tickets based on the information listed in our system.
If you believe this information is incorrect, please let us know.

(WE RESERVE THE RIGHT TO REFUSE SERVICE IF WE BELIEVE THE INFORMATION  BEING PROVIDED IS FRAUDULENT)

4) Clients must provide photo ID for any additional people they wish to pick up for

FAILURE TO PROVIDE ADDITIONAL ID WILL RESULT IN THAT ACCOUNT NOT BEING SERVED

5) Clients can only be served once every 7 days.

(PLEASE NOTE THE LAST DAY YOU RECEIVED FOOD AND PLAN AHEAD FOR THE FOLLOWING WEEK.)

6) Clients will only be given whatever food products are on the line at the time of their
service.

7) Please take note of posted operating hours. (They are listed on the entrance door,
our office door as well as on our website, google and all our social media).

CLIENTS WILL BE SERVED UP UNTIL POSTED CLOSING TIME.

ANY CLIENT COMING AFTER HOURS WILL BE ASKED TO RETURN DURING THE NEXT SERVICE DAY.

THERE ARE NO EXCEPTIONS TO THESE POLICIES

PLEASE FOLLOW THE DIRECTIONS OF STAFF AND MANAGEMENT AT ALL TIMES
FAILURE TO DO SO WILL RESULT IN YOU BEING DENIED SERVICE,

AND YOU WILL BE ASKED TO LEAVE, ALONG WITH BEING BLOCKED IN OUR SYSTEM.
IF YOU HAVE ANY QUESTIONS OR CONCERNS, PLEASE SEE A MEMBER OF MANAGEMENT

Elite Learners is now part of the New York Social Care Network (SCN) program.

The SCN (Social Care Network) program is a New York State initiative that connects Medicaid members to support for health-related social needs, including housing assistance, meal delivery, grocery support, pantry stocking, and transportation services.

I would like to introduce you to Betty Nicholas, who leads the SCN program. Betty will be a great resource and can provide additional information and support. All referrals of your clients who are on Medicaid should be directed to Betty for evaluation and assessment. You may reach her using the contact information below:

* Betty Nicolas
* Email: bnicolas@elitelearners.org
* Phone: 917-560-8935

**Eligibility for Food Assistance:**
To access food assistance, you will speak with a referral representative. They will ask a few questions to determine eligibility:
1.  Are you a Montgomery County resident?
2.  Does your household income meet Manna's eligibility guidelines?

**Scheduling Your Food Pick-Up:**
Please call Manna at (301) 424-1130 during business hours: Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM.
To ensure your food order is ready, please call by 12:00 PM the day before your desired pick-up date.

**What to Bring for Pick-Up:**
When picking up food, you must bring a photo ID with your current Montgomery County address, or another document verifying your county residency.

**Rescheduling Pick-Up:**
If you cannot pick up your food on the scheduled day, please call Manna to reschedule.

Our Client Choice Food Pantry is a modern, supermarket-style pantry where customers can shop for and choose the food they want. They can also reserve a time to visit the Food Pantry by using the mobile app Plentiful that allows customers to make appointments to shop. From July-October, our Food Pantry features fresh organic fruits and vegetables grown at local farms through United Way’s Local Produce Link program.


Our goal is to eliminate food insecurity by connecting clients to food and to resources and education they need to help lift them and their families out of crisis.


We are seeking volunteers  to help at the pantry. THSC Food Pantry is the only emergency food program in the area that is open four days a week, Tuesday – Friday, 9 am – 12 pm and 1– 4 pm.


How to make an appointment: 

A) Text "TRINITYHSC" to PANTRY (726-879) to make a reservation.

B) Apply online at http://www.plentifulapp.com

C) Call us at (718) 388-3176

D) Register on site in person with photo ID

You will need the following for everyone in your household who is applying:
A valid ID (i.e. driver’s license, birth certificate, government ID)
Proof of where you live (i.e. lease, rental agreement, utility bill)
*Unless you are experiencing homelessness
Proof of earned income (i.e. paystubs, employer letter, recent tax records)
Proof of unearned income (benefits award letter)
Proof of resources (i.e. bank statements)
Social Security Number
Proof of immigration status (for non-citizens)
Proof of student status (for college students)
You can get more benefits by providing:

Housing costs (i.e. rent receipt, mortgage statement, property tax bills)
Phone and utility costs
Medical expenses for anyone in your household 60+ or disabled 
Child and adult care costs
Child support paid by a person in your household

At 4MyCiTy, our focus is on the importance of environmental sustainability, primarily through the sustainable management of food to reduce organic waste. Our programs not only limit the harmful effects caused by organic waste on our environment but also enhance food security for families within the communities we serve. Additionally, we are committed to fostering economic and personal growth through our 4MyCiTy U! Job training and mentoring program, which equips participants with vital skills in sustainable practices and community service, further strengthening our community’s resilience and self-sufficiency.

Please Note - Our focus is on Environmental Sustainability, 4MyCiTy is not a food bank. We rescue food as part of our sustainability mission and make those rescued resources available to families within our communities. 


**Requirements**: Participate in both the compost and food program. For our compost program, we will provide compost bags. You just have to bring in your compost in the bag.

Hearts and Hands Food Pantry is a full choice food pantry that offers fresh and nonperishablel groceries, personal care items and pet foods **BY APPOINTMENT ONLY**. Appointments can be made from our website  https://www.heartsandhandsfoodpantry.com/get-help. We do not require referrals but welcome them. We offer two services, PICKUP and DELIVERY. PICKUP has less regulations than DELIVERY which is more stringent.

For PICKUP, an appointment must be made, the client must bring a photo ID (open to all not just US citizens) and a piece of mail as proof of address. For DELIVERY, client must meet 2 of our eligibility requirements and submit a photo ID and a verification document for their eligibility (for example: Disability benefits cover letter, doctor's note, etc). This information can be found in detail on our service pages through our website.
**Register for food delivery here** https://qualtricsxmxbndr96jl.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_50c5qbE7W7avDsW 

**NOTE:** When seeking an appointment through our website, please read everything carefully and understand it.

Two please be patient in line, and Gas Up, our line can get long, we serve 300 cars on a usual basis, sometime near 400, so Gas Up .. there is a process that we have to go through, each car will take a different amount of time , depending on how many families are being represented in that car.. And, Three, one person can pick up food for up to 4 families, BUT they must show us 3 more ID Pictures, usually on their phone, or some bring paper copies. Remember, each car takes time to collect the info., from a couple minutes to maybe 10 minutes ? We never know ..again please be patient. Last, We do offer free clothing ( inside set up) until they run out , after food you are welcome to go around the corner to our side door and come in .. We call this walletless shopping lol.. * You can come once a month for a full shop and once a month for produce (going forward it will be produce only.
* We will give out random numbers close to our start time to determine the order in which people shop (lottery system). We will no longer be first come first served.
* Customers coming in after the lottery will be given a number as they enter the building.
You will still need a photo ID and proof of address.
* If it’s your first visit of the month we will give you a blue number tag for a full shop. We will also give you a ticket that lets you know how much of each item you can take.
* If you’ve already visited the pantry this month we will give you a green number tag for produce.
* Only one person from your family can go into the shopping area. For safety reasons children are not allowed in the shopping area (except the produce only area).
* If you are a proxy picking up food for someone else you can only shop for one family at a time.
* Please bring your own reusable shopping bags. As many grocers are no longer giving out bags anymore, we don’t have enough.
* First week of the month is very busy. Come later in the month for faster service.
* If you see any ways we can improve as we make this change, please let us know.

MANNA clients must currently be battling or in care for a serious illness and, due to that illness, are at acute nutritional risk. Some nutrition indicators include:
* Recent unintentional weight loss
* Recent, extended hospitalization
* Start of new medical treatment (ex. chemotherapy, radiation, or hemodialysis)
* Recovery from surgery
* Wound care

There are two easy ways to apply for MANNA’s meal and nutrition counseling services:

**How to Receive Food Assistance**

To receive food from Manna Food Market, please call our main office at (301) 424-1130 during business hours, Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM.

During your call, a referral representative will ask a few simple questions to determine eligibility:
*   Are you a Montgomery County resident?
*   Does your income fall below the self-sufficiency threshold?

**Important:** Please call by 12:00 PM (noon) the day before you wish to receive food, so we can prepare your order.

**Food Pickup:**
When picking up your food, please bring:
*   A photo ID with your current Montgomery County address, OR
*   A document that proves your current residency in Montgomery County.

If you are unable to pick up your food on the scheduled day, you must call Manna to reschedule.

HOW DO YOU APPLY FOR BENEFITS? 
To apply for benefits, the head of household, a household member, or an authorized 
person representing the household may complete a paper application, apply online or by 
phone. A paper application can be received from your local DFCS office, from the DFCS 
website, or by calling the Customer Contact Center at 1-877-423-4746 to request that an 
application be mailed to your home address. You may also call the Customer Contact

Mobile Market Guidelines

BRING YOUR OWN REUSABLE BAG!
We have limited bags available (and they hold more groceries!)
ONLY TWO SHOPPERS AT A TIME!
Please wait until you receive a shopping basket to begin shopping.
LOOK AT THE LABELS!
The maximum number you can take of each item is written next to the label.
Example: APPLES (5) = take up to 5 apples
BAG YOUR OWN GROCERIES!
Once you are done shopping, please go to the table with your basket. We ask that you bag your own groceries and leave the basket on the table when you are done.
ONLY SHOP FOR ONE HOUSEHOLD AT A TIME!
If you are shopping for more than one household (i.e. a friend, family, neighbors, etc.), you will need to get back in line and make a new basket for each household.

Manna Food operates at USG Lot 2, located near the front of the BSE building. They will set up in the parking lot for distribution.

**Registration & Appointments:**
Registration is required to receive food assistance. Please call Manna's main office at (301) 424-1130 during regular business hours (Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM) to sign up.
*   **Important:** Your call must be received by 12:00 PM the day before you wish to pick up food to ensure your order is ready.
*   During your call, a referral representative will ask a few simple questions:
    *   Are you a Montgomery County resident?
    *   Does your household income fall below the level necessary for self-sufficiency?
    *   Which day and Manna distribution location is most convenient for you to pick up your food? (Manna operates multiple food distribution sites.)

**Parking Information:**
Participants may park in either Lot 5 or Lot 2. Please consult the USG Parking Map for detailed directions.

**Food Pickup Requirements:**
When picking up food, you must present a photo ID showing your current Montgomery County address, or provide another document that proves your current residency in the county.

**Rescheduling Pickups:**
If you are unable to pick up your food on your scheduled day, you **must** call Manna to reschedule your pickup.

Our online store opens every Thursday at 8 AM and closes once inventory is depleted. You can place only one order per week.

If items are listed as “Out of Stock,” it means supplies for those items have been fully depleted. Please check again the following Thursday when the store reopens.

To be added to our waiting list, please email the following information to info@theonlinefoodpantry.org: Full Name, Home Address, Number of People in Household, Phone Number, Email Address, and your current situation. Being added to the waiting list does not guarantee a food delivery.

If you place an order and receive an order number, your order is confirmed, and a representative will contact you within 24-48 hours. After your order is placed, you will receive a confirmation email. A representative will then call you to schedule your delivery.

If you have any food preferences or restrictions, please reply to your confirmation email to let us know.

Due to high demand, please be aware that callbacks and email responses may take a few days, and not all inquiries may be returned.

How to Apply
You must submit a SNAP application form and required documents to apply or recertify for SNAP benefits. You can use any of the following formats to apply and submit required documents: 
Online 
The quickest way to apply or recertify for SNAP benefits is online. You can use the ACCESS HRA website or the ACCESS HRA Mobile App to apply or recertify for SNAP benefits. You can submit documents via the “Document Upload” section of the mobile app. 
Download a SNAP application from HRA's website:
SNAP Benefits Application (LDSS-4826):http://otda.state.nyenet/ldss_eforms/eforms/4826A.pdf
In Person 
You can pick up and submit an application from any SNAP Walk-in Customer Service Center. Find a SNAP Walk-in Customer Service Center near you.
You can also visit a local Community Based Organization (CBO) for application assistance, eligibility screening, and to submit documents. Find a CBO near you. 
By Phone (DSS OneNumber) Call (718) 557-1399 Business Hours: Monday - Friday: 8 AM - 5 PM 
You can reach staff during business hours (except for City Holidays). You can get help in most languages. Automated Assistance is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
To Mail Completed Applications: 
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program 
O. Box 29008 
Brooklyn, NY 11202 
To Fax Completed Applications/ Documents: 
Fax your application form along with required documents to:
917-639-1111 – New application 
917-639-2544 – Recertification
If you need to submit documents after you already submitted your application form, fax the documents to 917-639-2544.

Community Servings reviews all applications for medical eligibility. Unfortunately, applications that do not meet medical eligibility cannot be enrolled for services.

- The Kosher Meals at Home program delivers meals to senior adults who keep kosher (or Halal) and live on the **Upper West Side** (from 50th Street to 125th Street) and the **Upper East Side** (from 50th Street to 96th Street).

We are a student-run food back for all community members and Rowan students. 

We primarily serve produce in the form of vegetables. Occasionally, we have pantry items and rarely dairy. NO MEAT.

We do not require any form of ID or liscence to recieve food. You do not have to register. 

We are located in Parking Lot D of Rowan University, directly across from Glassboro High School. Look for the green-tractor trailer in a university parking lot. 

The food bank is held entirely outdoors, but patrons wait in their cars. Patrons line up on the far end of the parking lot, away from the green tractor trailer. There will be a parking attendant to help you line up. Once service begins, two cars will be sent up at a time to recieve deliveries. 

**If you come in a car, you must get into the car line.** We have a seperate table for walkups, created for students living on campus without cars. If you park, wait for service to start, and then walk up, we will send you to the car line. 

If you are a walkup, and do not want to wait outside in poor weather conditions, you are welcome to wait inside Rowan Engineering Hall, located to our north. The Engineering hall also has bathrooms that are free to use. 

If you are are using RideShare to get to campus, you may use the walk-up line. 

**Any student or community member found parking and using the walkup line will not be permitted to get food.**

We operate every Friday from 9am-11am. We are open during winter and summer breaks. We are closed for major holidays, such as Christmas, Thanksgiving, and New Year's. **We do not start at 10am, the website's times are inaccurate.**

We close for temperatures below freezing and emergency weather conditions. 

We may start later than usual (between 10am-11am) as the delivery truck with produce is not always on time. Please be prepared to wait an hour or more to recieve food. **You may not cut in line if you have an early appointment or other obligation. Please plan wisely and expect the truck to be late.** Clients in line can expect to wait between 1-2 hours for their food, depending on when the client gets here, and when the truck gets here.

If you need to use the restroom before service starts, you are free to park your car in line and walk to the Engineering Hall. **Do not leave your car once service starts.** The line moves quickly, once we get going. Once the truck gets here, the volunteers need at least ten minutes to set up. Use this information to plan your bathroom visits. 

Beforce service each Friday morning, please call this number: 856-244-1166. By calling this number, a recording will play, informing you if we are open for service or not. We try to inform our clients the week before if we are closed the following, but sometimes, we may have to cancel unexpectedly in the middle of the week. This number is the best way to know if service for the week is cancelled or not. 

Volunteers will place produce items in either your trunk, back seat, or front seat. Please provide boxes for students to place items inside. Boxes are preffered, but bags are also welcome. The trunk is preffered for dropping off items, but your car's back seat and front seat are perfectly acceptable.

Mobile Market Guidelines

BRING YOUR OWN REUSABLE BAG!
We have limited bags available (and they hold more groceries!)
ONLY TWO SHOPPERS AT A TIME!
Please wait until you receive a shopping basket to begin shopping.
LOOK AT THE LABELS!
The maximum number you can take of each item is written next to the label.
Example: APPLES (5) = take up to 5 apples
BAG YOUR OWN GROCERIES!
Once you are done shopping, please go to the table with your basket. We ask that you bag your own groceries and leave the basket on the table when you are done.
ONLY SHOP FOR ONE HOUSEHOLD AT A TIME!
If you are shopping for more than one household (i.e. a friend, family, neighbors, etc.), you will need to get back in line and make a new basket for each household.

**SNAP**
New Jersey’s Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program, NJ SNAP, provides food assistance to families with low incomes to help them buy groceries through a benefits card accepted in most food retail stores and some farmers markets. Eligibility is set by several factors, such as income and resources. You can use SNAP benefits to stretch your food budget and buy nutritious foods that can keep you and your family healthy. Many college students are eligible for SNAP!
If you would like to learn more or get help with a SNAP application, contact pantry_run@newark.rutgers.edu with "SNAP Application Help" in the subject line.

The Arlington EATS Market is a safe space for anyone in Arlington who needs food. If you are an Arlington resident in need of food, you can shop for free once each week (defined as Sunday-Saturday). Foods include a variety of fresh produce, meat, dairy and canned and shelf stable food. Please note items will vary from week to week.

The Market is open for shopping by appointment. Drop-ins are not allowed during the first hour the Market is open, but can usually be accommodated after 10am on Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Saturdays and after 3pm on Mondays. However, priority will be given to those with appointments, so drop-ins may be required to wait a bit as space in the Market is limited.

Make your appointment by phone: Call Arlington EATS’ hotline at (339) 707-6761. Leave a message with your name and phone number and the date and approximate time you’d like to shop. You will receive a call back within a day or two.

Food will be distributed monthly on the 3rd Saturday of the month.
1. Please do not arrive before 9:00 am. Arriving too early causes backups on Beech Daly.
2. While waiting in line for food distribution, do not leave your vehicle unattended. 
3. You must be present to receive food. 
4. In the event of inclement weather, Helps will be closed (if the schools are closed or closing, we will, too). Check www.helpsontheway.org to confirm. 
5. In some cases, like heavy rain, the clothing bank may be closed. 
6. When utilizing the clothing bank, please remember that we service many families. Do not take more than 2 bags of clothes. If you have a special need, email us at www.helpsontheway.org  or speak with a volunteer. Anyone abusing this policy will be asked to leave and forfeit the clothing bank privilege.
7. Do not abuse our volunteers. Confrontation, swearing or physical threats will not be tolerated. You will be asked to leave. 
8. Be courteous to others while waiting in line. Do not confront, swear, or physically threaten other clients. This type of behavior will not be tolerated. You will be asked to leave. 
9. Do not toss cigarettes out of your window and do not litter while on our property. 
10. Our distribution is outside. Do not enter our buildings without approval. See a volunteer is you need additional assistance.
11. Based on social welfare (COVID) and economic conditions, policy and procedures are subject to change. In such cases, notification will be provided.

Community members must show proof that they reside in Edgewood or
Joppa, MD (21040 OR 21085) through a government-issued ID and a piece
of mail received in the last 30 days.
Community members must bring proof of income and be within 200% of the
poverty line. If they have proof of receiving disability (either SSI or SSDI),
medical assistance, housing vouchers, or food assistance, they do not have
to provide proof of income. In the event an individual does not have any
income or receive benefits, the individual must sign an affidavit of
understanding and work with our case manager to apply for benefits.
Individuals must show proof of dependents living in their home via one of
the following documents: birth certificates, social security cards, school
enrollment paperwork, or court documents. 
If multiple adults live at the same address, each adult must provide
documentation of eligibility. 
Individuals must go through the vetting process every 90 days. The
exception to this is if the individual has a permanent disability or
permanent fixed income, in which case they will be vetted every six
months.

Get Help Applying
SNAP provides food assistance to families and individuals who qualify to help buy groceries in most food retail stores and some farmers markets. Eligibility is set by several factors, such as income and resources. Not sure if you are eligible? Learn more about SNAP eligibility https://www.nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/apply/eligibility/ or find out if you and your family may be eligible for SNAP https://www.njhelps.gov/.

Need Help with Your Application? Ask a SNAP Navigator
Applying for SNAP can be confusing. A SNAP Navigator can help you apply and help you keep your benefits so you can get the food assistance you need.

SNAP Navigators will walk you through:

How SNAP works
Eligibility requirements
What documents you need to apply
The application process
How to keep your SNAP benefits
They also can answer questions and correct misconceptions about SNAP to help you decide if SNAP is right for you. This help is free and confidential.

Get Help Applying
SNAP provides food assistance to families and individuals who qualify to help buy groceries in most food retail stores and some farmers markets. Eligibility is set by several factors, such as income and resources. Not sure if you are eligible? Learn more about SNAP eligibility https://www.nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/apply/eligibility/ or find out if you and your family may be eligible for SNAP https://www.njhelps.gov/.
​
Need Help with Your Application? Ask a SNAP Navigator
Applying for SNAP can be confusing. A SNAP Navigator can help you apply and help you keep your benefits so you can get the food assistance you need.
​
SNAP Navigators will walk you through:
​
How SNAP works
Eligibility requirements
What documents you need to apply
The application process
How to keep your SNAP benefits
They also can answer questions and correct misconceptions about SNAP to help you decide if SNAP is right for you. This help is free and confidential.

SNAP Navigators will walk you through:

How SNAP works
Eligibility requirements

The application process
How to keep your SNAP benefits

Located in the heart of Greenwich Village, Greenwich House Center on the Square is a vibrant older adult center offering nutritious lunch meals, wellness programs, and a welcoming community for New Yorkers ages 60 and up. Operated by Greenwich House, the Center provides a safe and social environment where members can enjoy hot lunches, connect with peers, and access support that promotes healthy aging.

In addition to week day congregate lunch meals, Center on the Square offers health and fitness classes, arts and cultural programming, social services, and opportunities to stay active and engaged. Membership is free for adults 60+, and meals are available on a suggested contribution basis—no one is turned away for inability to pay.

Center on the Square is open Monday–Friday and is an important neighborhood resource for older adults seeking wellness, creativity, and connection.

To receive food assistance:

*   Please call Manna's main office at (301) 424-1130 between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM, Monday through Friday.
*   Calls must be received by 12:00 PM the day before your desired pickup to ensure your order is ready.
*   You will speak with a referral representative who will ask a few simple questions to confirm eligibility.

**Eligibility Requirements:**

*   Must be a Montgomery County resident.
*   Household income must be below the self-sufficiency threshold.

**Pickup Information:**

*   Food distribution takes place from our truck, located in the parking lot on the left side of the building.
*   When picking up food, please bring a photo ID with your current Montgomery County address, or a document proving current county residency.
*   If you are unable to pick up your food on the scheduled day, please call Manna to reschedule.

**OVERVIEW**
* DTA’s local office lobby is open for services, including new self-service options. Visit the services section on this page to learn about all the things you can do at a DTA office and ways to contact the Department.

**TRANSPORTATION**
* You can reach the vicinity of the DTA office via MBTA subway and bus routes.
* It is strongly recommended that you check with the MBTA's website to make sure you receive their updated routes. They offer a [Trip Planner](https://www.mbta.com/trip-planner?plan=gsQEZnJvbYTECGxhdGl0dWRlxADECWxvbmdpdHVkZcQAxARuYW1lxADEB3N0b3BfaWTEAMQCdG-ExAhsYXRpdHVkZcQAxAlsb25naXR1ZGXEAMQEbmFtZcQAxAdzdG9wX2lkxAA=), which enables users to get personalized recommendations. 

**SERVICES**
* DTA’s local office lobby is open for services, including new self-service options. You can use our phones, copiers, or kiosks to apply for benefits or manage your case. DTA staff and interpreters are available to assist you.

* If you need an EBT card, you can request one be sent to you via mail on the DTA Connect mobile app, DTAConnect.com or using the automated prompts on the DTA Assistance Line (877-382-2363). Most cards are delivered within 3-5 days. You can also visit one of our local offices to receive an EBT card if you need one more quickly. If you cannot visit an office, talk to our staff about other options.

* You can apply for SNAP, TAFDC, and EAEDC online at DTAConnect.com or over the phone on the DTA Assistance Line at 877-382-2363 (press 7) (help in over 100 languages). Learn all you can do to manage your case 24/7 without visiting a DTA office (e.g., submit documents, request letter showing your DTA benefits, etc.) at Mass.gov/ContactDTA.

Get Help Applying
SNAP provides food assistance to families and individuals who qualify to help buy groceries in most food retail stores and some farmers markets. Eligibility is set by several factors, such as income and resources. Not sure if you are eligible? Learn more about SNAP eligibility https://www.nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/apply/eligibility/ or find out if you and your family may be eligible for SNAP https://www.njhelps.gov/.

Need Help with Your Application? Ask a SNAP Navigator
Applying for SNAP can be confusing. A SNAP Navigator can help you apply and help you keep your benefits so you can get the food assistance you need.

SNAP Navigators will walk you through:

How SNAP works
Eligibility requirements
What documents you need to apply
The application process
How to keep your SNAP benefits
They also can answer questions and correct misconceptions about SNAP to help you decide if SNAP is right for you. This help is free and confidential.

Mission Family Support Circle (FSC) works to provide the type of support services that moves towards an ever-improving quality of life centered in a healthy and diverse community.

Organizational Profile Family Support Circle (FSC) was founded in 2001 by caring individuals who recognized the need for quality respite care and in-home support services for individuals with developmental disabilities, and youth development. The organization originally started in Philadelphia Pennsylvania and now in the Metro Atlanta Area. FSC is a 501 (c)(3), nonsectarian, social service agency that will benefit the community at large by enhancing the potential and quality of life of each person and families we serve. Within FSC’s person-centered philosophy, is a deep commitment to be providers of supports with specific expertise in youth development, respite care, in-home support and community access program for both youth and those individuals with Developmental Disabilities.

* There is an official Department of Transitional Assistance Kiosk at this location with access to the DTA Connect mobile application and website. [Learn what you can do through DTA Connect.](https://www.mass.gov/guides/how-to-contact-dta)


***NOTE:*** These locations are not staffed by DTA employees.

**OVERVIEW**
* DTA’s local office lobby is open for services, including new self-service options. Visit the services section on this page to learn about all the things you can do at a DTA office and ways to contact the Department.

**TRANSPORTATION**
* You can reach the vicinity of the DTA office via MBTA subway and bus routes.
* It is strongly recommended that you check with the MBTA's website to make sure you receive their updated routes. They offer a [Trip Planner](https://www.mbta.com/trip-planner?plan=gsQEZnJvbYTECGxhdGl0dWRlxADECWxvbmdpdHVkZcQAxARuYW1lxADEB3N0b3BfaWTEAMQCdG-ExAhsYXRpdHVkZcQAxAlsb25naXR1ZGXEAMQEbmFtZcQAxAdzdG9wX2lkxAA=), which enables users to get personalized recommendations. 

**SERVICES**
* DTA’s local office lobby is open for services, including new self-service options. You can use our phones, copiers, or kiosks to apply for benefits or manage your case. DTA staff and interpreters are available to assist you.

* If you need an EBT card, you can request one be sent to you via mail on the DTA Connect mobile app, DTAConnect.com or using the automated prompts on the DTA Assistance Line (877-382-2363). Most cards are delivered within 3-5 days. You can also visit one of our local offices to receive an EBT card if you need one more quickly. If you cannot visit an office, talk to our staff about other options.

* You can apply for SNAP, TAFDC, and EAEDC online at DTAConnect.com or over the phone on the DTA Assistance Line at 877-382-2363 (press 7) (help in over 100 languages). Learn all you can do to manage your case 24/7 without visiting a DTA office (e.g., submit documents, request letter showing your DTA benefits, etc.) at Mass.gov/ContactDTA.

Welcome to Aaron's Place Inc. – More Than Just a Food Pantry

At Aaron's Place Inc., we are more than just a food pantry and soup kitchen — we are a community hub dedicated to supporting and empowering individuals and families in need.

We offer a wide range of free programs and services, including:

Health Education: Classes on diabetes care, weight management, and overall wellness.

Cooking Classes: Learn how to prepare healthy meals, including special Juicing Classes.

"Mommy and Me" Classes: Fun, interactive sessions for moms and their little ones.

Workforce Development: Resume writing, job training skills, and employment support.

Senior Activities: Engaging programs to keep our seniors active and connected.

Rental Assistance & Homeless Prevention: Helping you stay safely housed.

Whether you need food, support, or a place to grow and learn, we're here to help.
Visit us, join a class, or reach out — you're not alone.

We provide short-term emergency assistance to residents of Forsyth and Stokes Counties who have a crisis situation and need rent/mortgage, utilities, food, or medication assistance.
We conduct phone interviews for assistance as well as in-person interviews. Please call our Client Services phone line in Winston-Salem at 336.724.7453 or in Kernersville at 336.996.5401 to see if we are able to assist you.

To be interviewed and possibly assisted with your crisis, please be prepared with the following information in printed form to show why you are in a financial crisis:

Picture ID
Proof of crisis (ex. proof of hospitalization, doctor’s note, car repair bill, etc.)
Proof of household income for last and current month
As many bills and receipts from last and current month as possible to show how the household money has been spent
Rent/mortgage, utility bill information (late notice, cut-off notice, and/or eviction notice)
Deposit information (If you have Section 8 housing, please bring proof of the inspection.)
If you receive disability benefits and have a payee assigned to you, the payee needs to be with you for the interview.
Written prescription or prescription bottle with refills still available if seeking medication assistance. We generally do not fill prescriptions for insured individuals and we do not fill any controlled substance prescriptions.
Hours of Operation
Services are provided on a first-come, first-served basis. Registration for interviews ends 45 minutes before the end of operating hours due to the time it takes to conduct an interview. Registration could end earlier depending on the number of people who come for serves that day.
Winston-Salem Office
Monday – Friday: 9:00 a.m. - Noon and 1:00 p.m. - 4:00 p.m.

Kernersville Office
Monday – Friday: 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m.
Tuesday and Thursday: 1:00 p.m. to 4:00 p.m.

My Brother’s Keeper cannot give out high-end electronic items like iPods, cell phones, or game systems.

To receive groceries delivered by a Common Cupboard Ally volunteer you must be a Union County Resident and available to receive food on Saturday mornings.
Secondly, to qualify you must be unable to get to one of our evening pantries due to a significant barrier such as:
You lack transportation or have unreliable transportation
You are a senior citizen
You can’t drive at night
You are a single parent with young children
You are disabled or care for someone who is
You work in the evenings
You have a specific dietary need
If you believe you meet these qualifications, reach out to our Cupboard Ally Program Manager, Alexis McDonald. She will talk with you further and see if we can help.

**Accessibility**
* The kiosk is located between the 2nd and 3rd floors (right beside the tax windows). Take the North elevator to floor 3 and then head to the tax windows. You will see the podium just behind a column. 

People who use wheelchairs can reach the kiosk from the building's first floor entrance from Congress Street near Faneuil Hall or the third floor entrance from City Hall Plaza at Government Center by taking the North elevator up to the 6th floor then taking the South elevator down to the 3rd floor.

**OVERVIEW**
* DTA’s local office lobby is open for services, including new self-service options. Visit the services section on this page to learn about all the things you can do at a DTA office and ways to contact the Department.

**TRANSPORTATION**
* You can reach the vicinity of the DTA office via MBTA subway and bus routes.
* It is strongly recommended that you check with the MBTA's website to make sure you receive their updated routes. They offer a [Trip Planner](https://www.mbta.com/trip-planner?plan=gsQEZnJvbYTECGxhdGl0dWRlxADECWxvbmdpdHVkZcQAxARuYW1lxADEB3N0b3BfaWTEAMQCdG-ExAhsYXRpdHVkZcQAxAlsb25naXR1ZGXEAMQEbmFtZcQAxAdzdG9wX2lkxAA=), which enables users to get personalized recommendations. 

**SERVICES**
* DTA’s local office lobby is open for services, including new self-service options. You can use our phones, copiers, or kiosks to apply for benefits or manage your case. DTA staff and interpreters are available to assist you.

* If you need an EBT card, you can request one be sent to you via mail on the DTA Connect mobile app, DTAConnect.com or using the automated prompts on the DTA Assistance Line (877-382-2363). Most cards are delivered within 3-5 days. You can also visit one of our local offices to receive an EBT card if you need one more quickly. If you cannot visit an office, talk to our staff about other options.

* You can apply for SNAP, TAFDC, and EAEDC online at DTAConnect.com or over the phone on the DTA Assistance Line at 877-382-2363 (press 7) (help in over 100 languages). Learn all you can do to manage your case 24/7 without visiting a DTA office (e.g., submit documents, request letter showing your DTA benefits, etc.) at Mass.gov/ContactDTA.

* If you need an EBT card, you can request one be sent to you via mail on the DTA Connect mobile app, DTAConnect.com or using the automated prompts on the DTA Assistance Line (877-382-2363). Most cards are delivered within 3-5 days. You can also visit one of our local offices to receive an EBT card if you need one more quickly. If you cannot visit an office, talk to our staff about other options.

* You can apply for SNAP, TAFDC, and EAEDC online at DTAConnect.com or over the phone on the DTA Assistance Line at 877-382-2363 (press 7) (help in over 100 languages). Learn all you can do to manage your case 24/7 without visiting a DTA office (e.g., submit documents, request letter showing your DTA benefits, etc.) at Mass.gov/ContactDTA.

SNAP provides food assistance to families and individuals who qualify to help buy groceries in most food retail stores and some farmers markets. Eligibility is set by several factors, such as income and resources. Not sure if you are eligible? Learn more about SNAP eligibility https://www.nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/apply/eligibility/ or find out if you and your family may be eligible for SNAP https://www.njhelps.gov/.

Need Help with Your Application? Ask a SNAP Navigator
Applying for SNAP can be confusing. A SNAP Navigator can help you apply and help you keep your benefits so you can get the food assistance you need.

SNAP Navigators will walk you through:

How SNAP works
Eligibility requirements
What documents you need to apply
The application process
How to keep your SNAP benefits
They also can answer questions and correct misconceptions about SNAP to help you decide if SNAP is right for you. This help is free and confidential.

**About SNAP:**
SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) provides food assistance to eligible families and individuals, enabling them to purchase groceries at most food retail stores and some farmers markets. Eligibility depends on several factors, such as income and resources.

**Need Help Applying for SNAP?**
Applying for SNAP can be complex. If you're unsure about your eligibility or need assistance with the application process, a **SNAP Navigator** can help. Our navigators provide free and confidential support to help you understand, apply for, and maintain your SNAP benefits.

**SNAP Navigators will guide you through:**
*   How SNAP works
*   Eligibility requirements
*   Necessary application documents
*   The entire application process
*   How to maintain your SNAP benefits

They can also answer your questions and clarify common misconceptions about SNAP, helping you decide if the program is right for you.

[Meet Our Team](https://www.rwjbh.org/why-rwjbarnabas-health-/social-impact/snap-navigator-program/meet-the-snap-navigator-team/)

The Massachusetts Immigrant & Refugee Advocacy Coalition Helpline provides important information and resources to **all Massachusetts residents**. While we cannot offer individual legal advice or representation, we can share guidance on a range of topics.

Our helpline offers:
*   Guidance on how to find information about your immigration court case or a pending immigration application
*   General information about immigration pathways and processes
*   Resources for immigrants
*   "Know Your Rights" information
*   Information on what to do after someone has been detained by immigration

Please note: If you are calling from out-of-state, we may not be able to provide referrals in your area.

**To contact the helpline:** You can schedule an online appointment for a phone call follow-up, or leave a voicemail.

IN-OFFICE SERVICES
**AFFORDABLE HOUSING COUNSELING**

**HOMEOWNERSHIP COUNSELING**
Every other Thursday, from 10AM to 3PM, our office hosts an experienced homeownership counselor to guide constituents with homeownership, foreclosure, and financing. This is provided in partnership with Neighborhood Housing Services of Bedford-Stuyvesant.
**IMMIGRATION LAW COUNSELING**

**CIVIL LAW COUNSELING**
Every other Thursday, from 10AM to 4PM, we have an experienced lawyer available to provide legal assistance on a wide range of issues, with a specialization in landlord and tenant matters.
**VETERANS BENEFITS ASSISTANCE**

Meals America operates locally within Montgomery County, PA, and proudly serves all 67 Pennsylvania counties, including Philadelphia, PA. We are dedicated to providing nutritious and complimentary meals for eligible seniors on Medicare and Medicaid.
We provide Medicaid meals delivery to seniors needing long-term meal assistance, those who need short-term meal assistance while in recovery, or those who enjoy healthy free meals that require minimal preparation time.
Do you like free healthy meals delivered to your doorstep? Find out if you are eligible for the Meals America meal assistance program at NO COST.
Are you disabled?
Are you on short-term disability?
Do you have Medicare/Medicaid or ANY other health insurance that offers a paid meal option?
Are you over 60?
If you answered “yes” to any of these questions, you may qualify for Meals America’s weekly meal delivery service at NO COST!
If you have questions about eligibility, call us today at (215) 399-5676.

**OVERVIEW**
* DTA’s local office lobby is open for services, including new self-service options. Visit the services section on this page to learn about all the things you can do at a DTA office and ways to contact the Department.

**TRANSPORTATION**
* You can reach the vicinity of the DTA office via MBTA subway and bus routes.
* It is strongly recommended that you check with the MBTA's website to make sure you receive their updated routes. They offer a [Trip Planner](https://www.mbta.com/trip-planner?plan=gsQEZnJvbYTECGxhdGl0dWRlxADECWxvbmdpdHVkZcQAxARuYW1lxADEB3N0b3BfaWTEAMQCdG-ExAhsYXRpdHVkZcQAxAlsb25naXR1ZGXEAMQEbmFtZcQAxAdzdG9wX2lkxAA=), which enables users to get personalized recommendations. 

**SERVICES**
* DTA’s local office lobby is open for services, including new self-service options. You can use our phones, copiers, or kiosks to apply for benefits or manage your case. DTA staff and interpreters are available to assist you.

* If you need an EBT card, you can request one be sent to you via mail on the DTA Connect mobile app, DTAConnect.com or using the automated prompts on the DTA Assistance Line (877-382-2363). Most cards are delivered within 3-5 days. You can also visit one of our local offices to receive an EBT card if you need one more quickly. If you cannot visit an office, talk to our staff about other options.

* You can apply for SNAP, TAFDC, and EAEDC online at DTAConnect.com or over the phone on the DTA Assistance Line at 877-382-2363 (press 7) (help in over 100 languages). Learn all you can do to manage your case 24/7 without visiting a DTA office (e.g., submit documents, request letter showing your DTA benefits, etc.) at Mass.gov/ContactDTA.

NJ SNAP is a nutrition assistance program to help individuals and families with low incomes buy the groceries they need to eat healthy with a benefits card accepted in most food retail stores and some farmers markets.

Eligibility is determined by several factors, such as income and resources.

Click https://www.nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/apply/eligibility/ to see who’s eligible for SNAP Benefits.

Find Income Guidelines Here https://www.nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/about/qualify/

What You’ll Need to Apply for SNAP Benefits

Identification (for everyone in the household, such as driver’s license, birth certificate, government ID).
Social Security Numbers (for everyone in the household).
Phone and Utility bills (gas, electric, oil, water).
Housing Costs (rent receipt/agreement, mortgage statement and/or tax bill).
Proof of Income (for everyone in the household, such as pay stubs, employer letter, tax records).
Proof of Resources (bank statements)
Medical Expenses (if 60 or older and/or disabled).
Child and Adult Care Expenses

If you or someone you know is interested in learning more about SNAP, eligibility, or need help with applying, contact our SNAP outreach team: snap@ycamp.org | 856-812-6091

Get Help Applying
SNAP provides food assistance to families and individuals who qualify to help buy groceries in most food retail stores and some farmers markets. Eligibility is set by several factors, such as income and resources. Not sure if you are eligible? Learn more about SNAP eligibility https://www.nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/apply/eligibility/ or find out if you and your family may be eligible for SNAP https://www.njhelps.gov/.

Need Help with Your Application? Ask a SNAP Navigator
Applying for SNAP can be confusing. A SNAP Navigator can help you apply and help you keep your benefits so you can get the food assistance you need.

SNAP Navigators will walk you through:

How SNAP works
Eligibility requirements
What documents you need to apply
The application process
How to keep your SNAP benefits
They also can answer questions and correct misconceptions about SNAP to help you decide if SNAP is right for you. This help is free and confidential.

The Love Fridge Chicago 

The Love Fridge is a Chicago-based initiative created to nourish our communities through mutual aid. Powered by kindness, generosity, and most importantly, love, we firmly believe that feeding oneself is not a privilege, but a right.

With food insecurity at an all-time high, keeping our communities fed is now more important than ever. Our volunteers come from all over Chicago, working within their own neighborhoods to collaborate with like-minded partners to place community refrigerators across the city. 

These refrigerators, painted by local artists to reflect the communities in which they live, provide neighbors the opportunity to donate food as well as take what they need, ensuring accessible food 24/7.
Open 24/7! Free, outdoor community fridge/pantry operated by local volunteers.

OVERVIEW: Offers assistance for seniors and individuals, 18 and older with disabilities. It is a statewide resource for information and assistance about long-term services and supports to allow individuals to remain at home and plan for future needs.  SERVICES: 1) AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER: Provides individuals who are seeking long term support information and counseling regarding available long term support options, and determining eligibility for public long term support programs and benefits. Benefits screening includes level of care determinations for Medicaid nursing facility and home and community-based service waiver programs.   2) SENIOR FARMERS' MARKET NUTRITION PROGRAM: Provides vouchers to low-moderate income older adults for use at local farmers' markets in the community. Vouchers are provided in the summer (usually in June). Call for more information on eligibility, distribution sites, days, and times. Find a market here.3) INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE: Offers in person assistance to individuals, families and caregivers with questions and/or concerns.4) INFORMATION AND REFERRAL: Connects individuals with information and referrals to other programs and benefits (such as health promotion and disease prevention, transportation services, housing and income support programs) that may help people remain in the community.

FOOD DISCOUNT: Partnership with the City of Baltimore, people who receive SNAP benefits can get up to $180 off produce. Participants receive $15 off select SNAP EBT fruit and vegetable participating products on pickup or delivery orders. Participants will need an account on Giantfood.com which requires their email address, physical address, and phone number. Must add $15 of select SNAP EBT fruit and vegetable participating products prior to checkout to receive offer. Limit 2 redemptions per household each calendar month. Order calculation excludes non-SNAP EBT qualifying products including alcohol, tobacco, gift cards, postage stamps and any other purchases prohibited by law. Offer not transferable or valid with any other offer. Standard delivery fees apply. Enter code GFBMOREFRESH1 at checkout while supplies last. Expires 10/13/23.
